takeaways: Senior living occupancy climbed to 87.4% in Q1 2026 with record 621,000 occupied units — Boston, Baltimore and Cincinnati all crossed 90%, signaling a supply-constrained runway for operators.

Seniors housing M&A hit 231 transactions in Q1 2026 (+25.5% YoY), anchored by Welltower's $4.6B Amica deal — capital is back, but disclosed dollar volume slid to $3.52B as confidential mega-deals took over.
Fed held rates at 3.50%–3.75% in March and markets now price the first cut no sooner than September — CRE borrowers should plan financing assumptions around 'higher-for-longer' through at least Q3.

items.headline: Senior living occupancy reaches 87.4% with record 621K occupied units in Q1
items.summary: NIC MAP reports Q1 2026 senior living occupancy rose 0.3 points to 87.4%, with independent living at 89% and assisted living at 85.8%. All 31 primary markets are above 80% occupancy and Boston (90.7%), Baltimore (90.6%) and Cincinnati (90.2%) crossed 90% for the first time since Q4 2019 — a strong demand signal for Lloyd Jones' senior housing portfolio.

items.headline: Welltower takes ownership of 9 Ontario properties in $4.6B Amica senior housing deal
items.summary: Welltower closed the first tranche of its $4.6 billion acquisition of Amica Senior Lifestyles, taking ownership of nine Ontario seniors housing properties in a roughly $1 billion sweep. The deal signals continued REIT appetite for high-end senior housing and sets a comparable pricing benchmark for private owner-operators.

items.headline: Seniors housing M&A hits 231 deals in Q1, up 25.5% year-over-year
items.summary: LevinPro LTC counted 231 publicly announced seniors housing and care transactions in Q1 2026, a 25.5% jump over Q1 2025 though down 19.8% from Q4's 288. Assisted living led at 46.8% of deals, skilled nursing at 36% — disclosed dollar volume fell to $3.52B as more deals closed confidentially.

items.headline: Fed holds rates at 3.50%–3.75%; first cut not expected before September
items.summary: The Federal Reserve held its benchmark rate at 3.50%–3.75% at the March 18 meeting, and futures markets now price the first cut no sooner than September or October 2026 — just one cut this year. The next FOMC decision is April 29; CRE underwriters should hold 'higher-for-longer' assumptions for refinancings.

items.headline: CRE capital markets poised for 15–20% transaction volume jump in 2026
items.summary: Colliers forecasts a 15–20% increase in CRE sales activity in 2026 as institutional and cross-border capital return, with the 10-year Treasury stabilized at 4.00%–4.25% and conventional commercial mortgage rates running 5.07%–8.75%. Debt markets are functioning with tight spreads and ample dry powder — a tailwind for Lloyd Jones' acquisition pipeline.

items.headline: Agentic AI moves from experiment to deployment across real estate workflows
items.summary: 2026 marks the shift from AI experimentation to agentic AI deployment, with real estate operators using autonomous agents for procurement, pricing, inventory, and event programming. Commercial real estate AI pilots jumped from 5% to 92% of companies in three years, with the proptech AI market projected to reach $159.9B by 2033.

items.headline: U.S. News names 1,614 Best Senior Living communities in 2026 ratings
items.summary: U.S. News & World Report on April 14 unveiled its 2026 Best Senior Living ratings, naming 1,614 communities after assessing more than 3,000. Bridge Senior Living earned 9 recognitions, with Fleming Farms in Huntsville, Ala. taking 12 honors. Residents and referral sources increasingly use these ratings — worth checking Lloyd Jones community placement.

items.headline: 80+ population projected to grow 25% in five years, double by 2035
items.summary: St. John's Leadership Team highlighted in Rochester Business Journal (April 10) that the 80-plus population will grow nearly 25% over the next five years and double by 2035. Demographic tailwinds, combined with constrained new supply, underpin the long-term occupancy runway for senior housing operators and investors.

items.headline: Apartment sales volume hit $165.5B in 2025, up 9.4% for second year of expansion
items.summary: MSCI Real Capital Analytics reported multifamily transaction volume of $165.5 billion for 2025, outpacing 2024 by 9.4% and marking the second consecutive year of expansion. Investors are targeting tight cap rates and anticipating stronger performance as debt capital loosens — constructive context for Lloyd Jones multifamily deployments.

items.headline: AI adoption in CRE focuses on tenant relationships, lease drafting, portfolio management
items.summary: Deloitte's 2026 CRE outlook identifies tenant relationship management, lease drafting, and portfolio management as the top AI use cases. AI tools are also streamlining compliance monitoring, document processing, and transaction security/fraud prevention — practical wins for operators looking to scale without adding headcount.

items.headline: Proptech 'monster rounds' return as valuations rebound in 2026
items.summary: High valuations and large financings are reappearing in proptech, with investors paying premiums for companies with proven distribution, durable scale, and category leadership. After a multi-year reset, capital is flowing back to vendors serving real estate operations, underwriting, and AI-driven workflows.

items.headline: Leaders anchor AI scaling to business outcomes, not individual tools
items.summary: Microsoft's April 2026 research finds the fastest-moving organizations are building systems for AI integration rather than chasing point solutions — tying AI directly to measurable business outcomes. For senior housing and CRE, that means prioritizing operations, leasing velocity, and resident-experience KPIs over shiny demos.

items.headline: 71% of leaders report increased stress; 40% considering leaving their jobs
items.summary: DDI reports 71% of leaders are under elevated stress and 40% are considering leaving their roles, with U.S. layoffs at a 10-year high entering 2026. Human-centered leadership — empathy, trust, emotional intelligence — is emerging as a top-ranked skill, with AI supporting rather than replacing human judgment.

items.headline: 89% of employees perform better when prioritizing wellbeing, Wellhub finds
items.summary: Wellhub's State of Work-Life-Wellness 2026 report finds 89% of employees perform better when their wellbeing is prioritized, reframing wellness as a productivity driver. For operators managing frontline staff in senior housing, the report reinforces that mental health support, flexible schedules, and preventive care pay off in retention and performance.

items.headline: 'Movement snacks' and longevity training go mainstream in 2026
items.summary: Short bursts of movement throughout the day — 'movement snacks' — are replacing the all-or-nothing workout mindset, with longevity training shifting from niche to mainstream. Senior living wellness programming that embeds micro-movement across daily routines aligns with the year's most durable lifestyle trend.

items.headline: Emotional fitness emerges as a new health category in 2026
items.summary: Dr. Axe and other wellness outlets identify 'emotional fitness' as a new 2026 category, pairing cognitive performance with emotional resilience. Adjacent trends — slow evenings, AI wellness coaches, biomarker-based nutrition — point to hyper-personal, data-driven programming that senior living communities can integrate into resident life.

items.headline: Strategy becomes a continuous learning system, not an annual artifact
items.summary: Barry O'Reilly and CEO Netweavers highlight that winning 2026 organizations treat strategy as a continuous learning system — iterating as conditions break — rather than producing annual static plans. For CRE and senior housing leadership, this favors shorter planning cycles and faster feedback loops on market conditions and operator performance.

items.headline: Northern Virginia senior living opens as luxury demand expands
items.summary: The Riviera, a luxury senior living building in Alexandria, Virginia, officially opened with a ribbon-cutting this month after first residents moved in last month. New luxury and upscale properties continue to open in high-income metro areas, reinforcing that Class A senior housing remains a priority for well-capitalized developers.

items.headline: Q1 2026 senior housing dollar volume falls 77% QoQ as confidential deals grow
items.summary: Disclosed Q1 2026 seniors housing M&A dollar volume came in at $3.52 billion, down 77% from Q4 2025's $15.32B and 40.3% below Q1 2025. Deal count stayed elevated at 231, suggesting a shift toward confidentially priced private-market transactions rather than a slowdown in activity.

items.headline: Employee wellness programs lean into preventive care and behavior change
items.summary: WellSteps' 2026 employer trends report emphasizes mental health support, flexible work policies, preventive care, and behavior-change programs over generic perks. The shift mirrors the broader move toward personalized, outcomes-based wellness that senior housing operators can bring to both staff and residents.
